channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senator Palwasha Khan urges NAB to handle all illegal land allotment cases
item.description: ISLAMABAD: PPP Senator Palwasha Khan on Monday criticised the Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) and National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA), calling them corrupt. She suggested that all ong...

item.title: Three Former Congress MLAs Join BJP in Assam Ahead of Assembly Elections
item.description: In a significant political development in Assam, three former Congress MLAs — Kamalakhya Dey Purkayastha, Basanta Das, and Sashi Kanta Das — joined the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) on Thursday....

item.title: Mary Fowler starts as Matildas beat Iran 1-0 in Asian Cup opener
item.description: The Matildas kicked off their second match of the 2026 Women’s Asian Cup against Iran at Cbus Super Stadium on the Gold Coast. Mary Fowler returned from an ACL injury to start alongside captain Sam Ke...

item.title: Edmonton Oilers Acquire Jason Dickinson and Colton Dach from Chicago Blackhawks for Andrew Mangiapane and Conditional 2027 First-Round Pick
item.description: The Edmonton Oilers have made another trade with the Chicago Blackhawks, acquiring centre Jason Dickinson and forward Colton Dach in exchange for winger Andrew Mangiapane and a conditional first-round...

item.title: Cyprus expresses concern over UK's handling of drone attacks at RAF Akrotiri
item.description: The Cypriot government has expressed concern over the UK's handling of recent drone attacks targeting RAF Akrotiri, particularly regarding the level of information shared with residents. Cypriot High ...

item.title: Abia Governor Inaugurates Upgraded Primary Health Centres Across State
item.description: Abia State Governor Alex Otti don continue with him plan to improve healthcare for people as e inaugurate another Primary Health Centre for Alaukwu Ward inside Umuagu, Isiala Ngwa South LGA under Proj...

item.title: Government finalises 28th Constitutional Amendment draft amid consensus talks
item.description: Adviser to the Prime Minister on Political and Public Affairs, Rana Sanaullah, announced in Chiniot that the government is in the final stages of preparing the 28th Constitutional Amendment. He stated...

item.title: Philippine Military and Defense Personnel Targeted by Foreign Espionage
item.description: The Philippines has uncovered instances where young, financially insecure, and impressionable government employees were recruited by Chinese nationals to access sensitive security and defense informat...

item.title: Malacañang Urges Government Employees to Safeguard Sensitive Data Amid Uncovered Chinese-Linked Espionage
item.description: Malacañang Palace, through Undersecretary Claire Castro, issued a reminder on March 5, 2026, calling on all government workers to stay careful and critical in handling information. This advisory follo...

item.title: Unregistered contractor behind Johannesburg building collapse that killed nine
item.description: The recent building collapse in Johannesburg’s Ormonde area, which tragically killed nine workers, has revealed serious regulatory failures. The company responsible, New World Order Investments 90 Ltd...

item.title: ECP schedules December 9 Senate by-election in Punjab after Irfan Siddiqui’s death
item.description: The Election Commission of Pakistan (ECP) has officially announced the schedule for a by-election to fill the Senate seat that became vacant after the passing of Pakistan Muslim League-Nawaz (PML-N) s...

item.title: Halifax student studies online after repeated antisemitic harassment at school
item.description: A 15-year-old Jewish student in Halifax, Nova Scotia, has been forced to study remotely after enduring prolonged antisemitic harassment at Park West School, according to his mother, Aviva Rubin-Schnei...

item.title: Tanga leaders warn children are overworked and need more play and rest
item.description: In Tanga, government leaders have expressed deep concern over the increasing pressures facing children, warning that many are being denied basic rights to play, rest, and grow in a healthy environment...

item.title: US Submarine Torpedoes Iranian Frigate IRIS Dena in Indian Ocean, First Submarine Torpedo Sinking Since WWII
item.description: On March 4, 2026, a U.S. Navy attack submarine torpedoed and sank the Iranian Navy frigate IRIS Dena in international waters in the Indian Ocean, approximately 40 nautical miles off the southern coast...

item.title: Claims of US Navy Using Indian Ports Amid Iran Tensions Denied by New Delhi
item.description: A former senior adviser to the US Secretary of Defence, retired Colonel Douglas Macgregor, claimed in an interview on One America News that the US Navy is increasingly depending on Indian ports for do...

item.title: Final Extension Announced for Hajj 2026 Second Instalment Payment
item.description: The Ministry of Religious Affairs and Interfaith Harmony has announced a final three-day extension for submission of Hajj 2026 dues, urging all applicants to pay their second instalment by Wednesday. ...

item.title: TMC Emphasises Bengali Identity Ahead of 2026 West Bengal Elections
item.description: As West Bengal prepares for its 2026 assembly elections, the ruling Trinamool Congress (TMC) has adopted a campaign deeply rooted in identity politics. Central to their strategy is the promotion of Be...

item.title: Spotlight Highlights Gallo Music Africa Centenary, Local Film Release and Family Animation
item.description: Spotlight brings South African audiences a digest of the latest in entertainment. This week, the feature focuses on three major highlights. Firstly, the local film 'The Heart Is a Muscle,' written and...

item.title: Byelection for Farrer seat set for May 9 with major parties and independents contesting
item.description: The byelection for the Farrer seat in regional New South Wales, vacated by former opposition leader Sussan Ley, is scheduled for Saturday, 9 May. The Speaker of the House of Representatives announced ...

item.title: Iranian fans highlight oppression of players at Women’s Asian Cup in Australia
item.description: During the opening match of the Women’s Asian Cup at Gold Coast Stadium, Iranian players remained silent during their national anthem, reflecting the severe restrictions and threats imposed on them by...
